material_overrides: Fee deferral: DFPI imposed deferral of all initial fees until pre-opening obligations completed and franchisee open for business
Noncompete post-term: May not be enforceable under CA law
Governing law: LA law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
Maximum interest rate: 10% annually
Bankruptcy termination: May not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
Franchise Agreement sections deleted: 12.G, 12.H, 18.A.2.c (bolded paragraph), 37.D, portions of 46 (integration/disclaimer clauses)
Anti-waiver: No questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive claims under state franchise law
Registration filing disclosure (standard)
Franchise registration history: MN and SD cancelled in 2008 for late renewal; registrations lapsed in CA, HI, IL, ND, RI, WA, WI in 2008
CT registration withdrawn (trademark exemption pending)
Governing law: Illinois law governs
Venue: Designation outside Illinois is void per IL Franchise Disclosure Act Section 4
Financial condition: Explicitly flags financial condition concern per Item 21
Anti-waiver: Standard
General release on renewal/transfer: Does not apply to claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
Claims period: Must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise (MD law)
Forum: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for MD law claims
Anti-waiver of MD franchise law rights
Integration clause disclaimers deleted (same as CA)
Trademark protection: Franchisor must protect franchisee's right to use marks and indemnify
Release on renewal: No release language relieves franchisor from MN franchise law liability
Termination notice: 90 days notice with 60 days to cure; 180 days for non-renewal
Injunction: Modified to 'consent to seeking' rather than automatic consent
Liquidated damages: Section 37 H.3 deleted in its entirety
Anti-waiver: Standard
Standard NY registration disclosures (Items 3, 4, 5)
General release: Rights under NY GBL Article 33 preserved (non-waiver per GBL 687.4, 687.5)
Injunction provisions: Modified to 'may seek to have' rather than automatic
Specific performance language modified to require seeking rather than automatic grant
Governing law: ND law supersedes LA law where conflicting
Venue: All provisions designating venue outside ND are deleted
General release: Not required on renewal
Noncompete: Generally considered unenforceable in ND per Section 9-08-06
Liquidated damages: Section 37 H.3 deleted entirely
Jury trial waiver: Deleted
Punitive damages waiver: Deleted
Statute of limitations: ND law applies (not 1-year contractual limit)

states_filed: CA, HI, IL, IN, ME, MD, MI, MN, NY, NC, ND, RI, SC, SD, VA, WA, WI
notes: Michigan disclosures appear in front matter (pages 5-6) rather than Exhibit E. Indiana, Maine, North Carolina, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Virginia, Washington, Wisconsin addenda may be present but not individually identified in this pass.
